```{r cox_zurich_cindex_table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
m1 <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ eta,            data = df_surv_z, x = TRUE)
m2 <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ COMPERA_number + eta, data = df_surv_z, x = TRUE)

c0 <- unname(summary(m0)$concordance[1])
c1 <- unname(summary(m1)$concordance[1])
c2 <- unname(summary(m2)$concordance[1])

cindex_tbl <- data.frame(
  model   = c("COMPERA", "Score", "COMPERA + score"),
  c_index = c(c0, c1, c2)
)

cindex_tbl

# Likelihood ratio test: does adding score improve COMPERA?
lrt <- anova(m0, m2, test = "LRT")
lrt
```


This gives you exactly the table format you showed (just with “Score” instead of “LASSO score” — rename if you want).

---

## Optional (recommended) — bootstrap CI for c-index difference (COMPERA vs COMPERA+score)

With small cohorts/events, c-index can be noisy. This gives you a quick sense of stability.

```{r cox_zurich_cindex_bootstrap, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
set.seed(1)
B <- 500

boot_delta <- replicate(B, {
  idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(df_surv_z)), replace = TRUE)
  d <- df_surv_z[idx, , drop = FALSE]

  # handle rare bootstrap samples with 0 events
  if (length(unique(d$event)) < 2) return(NA_real_)

  m0b <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ COMPERA_number, data = d)
  m2b <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ COMPERA_number + eta, data = d)

  c0b <- unname(summary(m0b)$concordance[1])
  c2b <- unname(summary(m2b)$concordance[1])
  c2b - c0b
})

boot_delta <- boot_delta[is.finite(boot_delta)]
quantile(boot_delta, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))
mean(boot_delta)
```


---

## Optional — model summary table (HRs)
If you want a quick HR table for the combined model:

```{r cox_zurich_hr_table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(broom)

tidy(m2, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
```

```{r plot_boot_delta, fig.height=3.6, fig.width=5.5}
library(ggplot2)
df_bd <- data.frame(delta_c = boot_delta)

ggplot(df_bd, aes(x = delta_c)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  labs(x = "Δ C-index (COMPERA+score − COMPERA)",
       y = "Count",
       title = "Bootstrap distribution of c-index improvement") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

```

```{r}
cox.zph(m2)
```

## Suggested figures for COMPERA and mortality results

These chunks assume the objects from your final-part analysis already exist, especially:
- `clin_z`, `pred_z`, `df_surv_z`
- `m0`, `m1`, `m2`, `cindex_tbl`
- `output_dir_figs`

### 1) Final score across COMPERA risk strata

This is probably the clearest figure for the COMPERA-related result.
It shows whether the final score increases across clinical risk strata.

```{r score_by_compera_boxplot, fig.height=4.3, fig.width=5.8, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

score_by_compera <- clin_z %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, COMPERA_4strata, COMPERA_number) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(pred_z %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, score_eta), by = "Barcode") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    COMPERA_4strata = factor(
      COMPERA_4strata,
      levels = c("low", "intermediate-low", "intermediate-high", "high")
    ),
    COMPERA_number = as.numeric(COMPERA_number)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(COMPERA_4strata), !is.na(COMPERA_number), !is.na(score_eta))

p_kw <- kruskal.test(score_eta ~ COMPERA_4strata, data = score_by_compera)$p.value
rho   <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(score_by_compera$COMPERA_number, score_by_compera$score_eta, method = "spearman"))

cols_compera <- c(
  "low"               = "#9CA3AF",
  "intermediate-low"  = "#DDB7B2",
  "intermediate-high" = "#C7685F",
  "high"              = "#A63A2A"
)

ggplot(score_by_compera, aes(x = COMPERA_4strata, y = score_eta, fill = COMPERA_4strata)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_compera) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1,
    y = max(score_by_compera$score_eta, na.rm = TRUE),
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1.2,
    size = 4,
    label = paste0(
      "Kruskal-Wallis p = ", signif(p_kw, 3),
      "\nSpearman rho = ", round(unname(rho$estimate), 2),
      ", p = ", signif(rho$p.value, 3)
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Final score across COMPERA risk strata",
    x = "COMPERA risk stratum",
    y = "Final score (linear predictor)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```


### 2) Trend plot across COMPERA strata

This is a nice companion to the boxplot if you want to emphasize monotonic increase.

```{r score_by_compera_trend, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=4.6, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

trend_df <- score_by_compera %>%
  dplyr::group_by(COMPERA_number, COMPERA_4strata) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_score = mean(score_eta, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_score   = sd(score_eta, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
    n = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(trend_df, aes(x = COMPERA_number, y = mean_score)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_score - se_score, ymax = mean_score + se_score), width = 0.08) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = trend_df$COMPERA_number, labels = trend_df$COMPERA_4strata) +
  labs(
    title = "Final score increases with COMPERA risk",
    x = "COMPERA risk stratum",
    y = "Mean final score ± SE"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
```


### 3) Forest plot for Cox regression results

Yes: for the mortality results, a forest plot is one of the best ways to show the Cox model output.
This chunk shows HRs for:
- COMPERA alone
- score alone
- combined model

```{r cox_forest_plot, fig.height=4.6, fig.width=6.6, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)

forest_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  broom::tidy(m0, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% dplyr::mutate(model = "COMPERA only"),
  broom::tidy(m1, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% dplyr::mutate(model = "Score only"),
  broom::tidy(m2, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% dplyr::mutate(model = "COMPERA + score")
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term %in% c("COMPERA_number", "eta")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    term_label = dplyr::case_when(
      term == "COMPERA_number" & model == "COMPERA only"     ~ "COMPERA",
      term == "eta"            & model == "Score only"       ~ "Score",
      term == "COMPERA_number" & model == "COMPERA + score" ~ "COMPERA (adjusted for score)",
      term == "eta"            & model == "COMPERA + score" ~ "Score (adjusted for COMPERA)",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    term_label = factor(
      term_label,
      levels = rev(c(
        "Score (adjusted for COMPERA)",
        "COMPERA (adjusted for score)",
        "Score",
        "COMPERA"
      ))
    )
  )

ggplot(forest_df, aes(x = estimate, y = term_label)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.18, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Cox regression for mortality",
    x = "Hazard ratio (log scale)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
```


### 4) Kaplan-Meier plot by final score group

This is the other figure I would definitely include for mortality.
Use a median split to avoid overfitting and to keep the figure simple.

```{r km_by_score_group, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=5.6, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(survival)

km_df <- df_surv_z %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    score_group = ifelse(eta >= median(eta, na.rm = TRUE), "High score", "Low score"),
    score_group = factor(score_group, levels = c("Low score", "High score"))
  )

fit_km <- survival::survfit(Surv(time_days, event) ~ score_group, data = km_df)
logrank_p <- 1 - pchisq(survival::survdiff(Surv(time_days, event) ~ score_group, data = km_df)$chisq, df = 1)

plot(
  fit_km,
  col = c("#004C8C", "#A63A2A"),
  lwd = 2,
  xlab = "Follow-up (days)",
  ylab = "Survival probability",
  main = "Kaplan-Meier survival by final score"
)
legend(
  "bottomleft",
  legend = c(
    paste0("Low score (n=", sum(km_df$score_group == "Low score"), ")"),
    paste0("High score (n=", sum(km_df$score_group == "High score"), ")")
  ),
  col = c("#004C8C", "#A63A2A"),
  lwd = 2,
  bty = "n"
)
mtext(paste0("Log-rank p = ", signif(logrank_p, 3)), side = 3, adj = 1, line = 0.2, cex = 0.9)
```


### 5) C-index comparison plot

This is better than leaving the c-index only as a printed table.

```{r cindex_dotplot, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=5.2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)

cindex_tbl$model <- factor(cindex_tbl$model, levels = c("COMPERA", "Score", "COMPERA + score"))

ggplot(cindex_tbl, aes(x = c_index, y = model)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", c_index)), nudge_x = 0.015, size = 4) +
  xlim(0.5, 1.0) +
  labs(
    title = "Model discrimination",
    x = "C-index",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
```


### 6) Optional: bootstrap distribution of C-index improvement

This is useful if you want to support the claim that adding the score improves prediction over COMPERA.

```{r delta_cindex_bootstrap_plot, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=5.5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)

df_bd <- data.frame(delta_c = boot_delta)
df_bd <- df_bd[is.finite(df_bd$delta_c), , drop = FALSE]

ggplot(df_bd, aes(x = delta_c)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Bootstrap distribution of C-index improvement",
    x = expression(Delta*" C-index (COMPERA + score minus COMPERA)"),
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
```


### 7) Optional: save the important figures

```{r save_compera_mortality_figures, eval=FALSE}
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "Score_by_COMPERA_boxplot.png"), width = 5.8, height = 4.3, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "Score_by_COMPERA_trend.png"), width = 4.6, height = 3.8, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "Cox_forest_plot.png"), width = 6.6, height = 4.6, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "Cindex_dotplot.png"), width = 5.2, height = 3.8, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "Delta_Cindex_bootstrap.png"), width = 5.5, height = 3.8, dpi = 300)

png(here::here(output_dir_figs, "KM_by_score_group.png"), width = 5.6, height = 4.5, units = "in", res = 300)
plot(
  fit_km,
  col = c("#004C8C", "#A63A2A"),
  lwd = 2,
  xlab = "Follow-up (days)",
  ylab = "Survival probability",
  main = "Kaplan-Meier survival by final score"
)
legend(
  "bottomleft",
  legend = c(
    paste0("Low score (n=", sum(km_df$score_group == "Low score"), ")"),
    paste0("High score (n=", sum(km_df$score_group == "High score"), ")")
  ),
  col = c("#004C8C", "#A63A2A"),
  lwd = 2,
  bty = "n"
)
mtext(paste0("Log-rank p = ", signif(logrank_p, 3)), side = 3, adj = 1, line = 0.2, cex = 0.9)
dev.off()
```

## Additional figures: Poland COMPERA score and cohort severity comparison

These chunks assume that the main analysis has already been run and that the following objects already exist:
- `clin_z`, `clin_p`
- `pred_z`, `pred_p`
- `output_dir_figs`

They add:
1. Final score across COMPERA strata in the Poland cohort
2. Final score across COMPERA strata in both cohorts (optional combined figure)
3. TAPSE/sPAP comparison between Zurich and Poland in SSc-PAH patients
4. COMPERA group counts in the two cohorts shown as side-by-side barplots

```{r helper_compera_labels, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

standardize_compera <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- trimws(tolower(x))
  dplyr::case_when(
    x %in% c("low") ~ "low",
    x %in% c("intermediate-low", "intermediate low", "intermediate_low") ~ "intermediate-low",
    x %in% c("intermediate-high", "intermediate high", "intermediate_high") ~ "intermediate-high",
    x %in% c("high") ~ "high",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

compera_levels <- c("low", "intermediate-low", "intermediate-high", "high")
compera_labels <- c("low", "intermediate-low", "intermediate-high", "high")
compera_cols <- c(
  "low"               = "#9CA3AF",
  "intermediate-low"  = "#DDB7B2",
  "intermediate-high" = "#C7685F",
  "high"              = "#A63A2A"
)
cohort_cols <- c("Zurich" = "#004C8C", "Poland" = "#A63A2A")
```

### 1) Poland cohort: final score across COMPERA strata

```{r poland_score_by_compera, fig.height=4.4, fig.width=5.8, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
score_poland_compera <- clin_p %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, Compera_4strata, Compera_number) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(pred_p %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, score_eta), by = "Barcode") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    COMPERA = standardize_compera(Compera_4strata),
    COMPERA = factor(COMPERA, levels = compera_levels),
    Compera_number = as.numeric(Compera_number)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(COMPERA), !is.na(Compera_number), !is.na(score_eta))

p_kw_pol <- kruskal.test(score_eta ~ COMPERA, data = score_poland_compera)$p.value
rho_pol  <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(score_poland_compera$Compera_number,
                                      score_poland_compera$score_eta,
                                      method = "spearman"))

ggplot(score_poland_compera, aes(x = COMPERA, y = score_eta, fill = COMPERA)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = compera_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1,
    y = max(score_poland_compera$score_eta, na.rm = TRUE),
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1.2,
    size = 4,
    label = paste0(
      "Kruskal-Wallis p = ", signif(p_kw_pol, 3),
      "\nSpearman rho = ", round(unname(rho_pol$estimate), 2),
      ", p = ", signif(rho_pol$p.value, 3)
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Poland cohort: final score across COMPERA risk strata",
    x = "COMPERA risk stratum",
    y = "Final score (linear predictor)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

### 2) Optional combined figure: final score across COMPERA strata in both cohorts

```{r both_cohorts_score_by_compera, fig.height=4.6, fig.width=8.5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
score_zurich_compera <- clin_z %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, COMPERA_4strata, COMPERA_number) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(pred_z %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, score_eta), by = "Barcode") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    COMPERA = standardize_compera(COMPERA_4strata),
    COMPERA = factor(COMPERA, levels = compera_levels),
    COMPERA_number = as.numeric(COMPERA_number),
    Cohort = "Zurich"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(COMPERA), !is.na(COMPERA_number), !is.na(score_eta))

score_poland_compera2 <- clin_p %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, Compera_4strata, Compera_number) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(pred_p %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, score_eta), by = "Barcode") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    COMPERA = standardize_compera(Compera_4strata),
    COMPERA = factor(COMPERA, levels = compera_levels),
    Compera_number = as.numeric(Compera_number),
    Cohort = "Poland"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(COMPERA_number = Compera_number) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(COMPERA), !is.na(COMPERA_number), !is.na(score_eta))

score_both_compera <- dplyr::bind_rows(score_zurich_compera, score_poland_compera2)

ggplot(score_both_compera, aes(x = COMPERA, y = score_eta, fill = COMPERA)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.75) +
  facet_wrap(~Cohort) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = compera_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Final score across COMPERA risk strata",
    x = "COMPERA risk stratum",
    y = "Final score (linear predictor)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

### 3) TAPSE/sPAP comparison between cohorts in SSc-PAH patients

```{r tapse_spap_boxplot_by_cohort, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=5.8, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
tapse_spap_compare <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  clin_z %>%
    dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Barcode,
      TAPSE_sPAP = as.numeric(TAPSE_sPAP),
      Cohort = "Zurich"
    ),
  clin_p %>%
    dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Barcode,
      TAPSE_sPAP = as.numeric(TAPSE_sPAP),
      Cohort = "Poland"
    )
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(TAPSE_sPAP))

p_tapse <- wilcox.test(TAPSE_sPAP ~ Cohort, data = tapse_spap_compare)$p.value

ggplot(tapse_spap_compare, aes(x = Cohort, y = TAPSE_sPAP, fill = Cohort)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.32, linetype = 2, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cohort_cols) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.5,
    y = max(tapse_spap_compare$TAPSE_sPAP, na.rm = TRUE),
    label = paste0("Wilcoxon p = ", signif(p_tapse, 3)),
    vjust = 1.2,
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "TAPSE/sPAP in SSc-PAH patients",
    x = NULL,
    y = "TAPSE/sPAP"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

### 4) COMPERA group distribution by cohort (counts)

This gives two barplots next to each other, one for Zurich and one for Poland, with patient counts per risk group.

```{r compera_counts_by_cohort, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=8, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
compera_counts <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  clin_z %>%
    dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Cohort = "Zurich",
      COMPERA = standardize_compera(COMPERA_4strata)
    ),
  clin_p %>%
    dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Cohort = "Poland",
      COMPERA = standardize_compera(Compera_4strata)
    )
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(COMPERA)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(COMPERA = factor(COMPERA, levels = compera_levels)) %>%
  dplyr::count(Cohort, COMPERA, .drop = FALSE)

ggplot(compera_counts, aes(x = COMPERA, y = n, fill = COMPERA)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.25, size = 4) +
  facet_wrap(~Cohort, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = compera_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "COMPERA risk-group distribution in SSc-PAH patients",
    x = "COMPERA risk stratum",
    y = "Number of patients"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)
  )
```

### 5) Optional: percentages instead of counts

If you also want a cleaner comparison independent of cohort size, this percentage version can be useful in supplement.

```{r compera_percent_by_cohort, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=8, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
compera_props <- compera_counts %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Cohort) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

ggplot(compera_props, aes(x = COMPERA, y = prop, fill = COMPERA)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(prop, accuracy = 1)), vjust = -0.25, size = 4) +
  facet_wrap(~Cohort, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = compera_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1.05)) +
  labs(
    title = "COMPERA risk-group proportions in SSc-PAH patients",
    x = "COMPERA risk stratum",
    y = "Proportion of patients"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

### 6) Optional save commands

```{r save_poland_and_cohort_comparison_figures, eval=FALSE}
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "Poland_score_by_COMPERA.png"), width = 5.8, height = 4.4, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "Both_cohorts_score_by_COMPERA.png"), width = 8.5, height = 4.6, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "TAPSE_sPAP_by_cohort_PAH.png"), width = 5.8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "COMPERA_counts_by_cohort.png"), width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "COMPERA_percent_by_cohort.png"), width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
```

```{r poland_score_by_death_status, fig.height=4.2, fig.width=4.8, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

death_col_p <- "Death (no/yes; 0/1)"

score_poland_death <- clin_p %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, all_of(death_col_p)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(pred_p %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, score_eta), by = "Barcode") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    death = as.numeric(.data[[death_col_p]]),
    death_status = dplyr::case_when(
      death == 0 ~ "No",
      death == 1 ~ "Yes",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    death_status = factor(death_status, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(death_status), !is.na(score_eta))

score_poland_death %>%
  dplyr::count(death_status)

p_death_pol <- wilcox.test(score_eta ~ death_status, data = score_poland_death)$p.value

ggplot(score_poland_death, aes(x = death_status, y = score_eta, fill = death_status)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("No" = "grey75", "Yes" = "#A63A2A")) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.5,
    y = max(score_poland_death$score_eta, na.rm = TRUE),
    label = paste0("Wilcoxon p = ", signif(p_death_pol, 3)),
    vjust = 1.2,
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Poland cohort: final score by death status",
    subtitle = "SSc-PAH patients only",
    x = "Death",
    y = "Final score (linear predictor)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
